pattern: Re-rank each letter by its position in the custom alphabet, then check that every adjacent pair of words is in non-decreasing order under that ranking, treating a shorter prefix as smaller.
hints: The only thing that changes is which letter counts as 'smaller'; build a lookup from each letter to its position in the given order.
Sorted-ness is a local property: the whole list is sorted exactly when each word is less than or equal to the one right after it.

Compare two neighbouring words character by character using the ranks; at the first differing position the smaller rank must come first, and if one word is a prefix of the other the shorter one must come first.

edge_inputs.description: two words ordered correctly under a remapped alphabet
a later word should come first under the custom order
a longer word followed by its own prefix is out of order
prefix before the longer word is correctly ordered
reversed alphabet makes a normally-sorted list unsorted
single-letter words sorted by custom order

stress.description: random alphabets and word lists, frequently built to be already sorted so both true and false outcomes appear
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    letters = list(string.ascii_lowercase)
    rng.shuffle(letters)
    order = ''.join(letters)
    rank = {c: i for i, c in enumerate(order)}
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100)))
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        wl = rng.randint(1, 6)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice(order) for _ in range(wl)))
    if rng.random() < 0.6:
        words.sort(key=lambda w: [rank[c] for c in w])
    return [words, order]
stress.note: sorting under the custom rank yields valid 'true' cases; unsorted random lists exercise 'false'
